---
title: "Appendix 3: Scenario 3 - Individual Stock Perspective (TSLA vs KO)"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-21"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(glmnet)
library(randomForest)
library(zoo)
library(caret)
library(Metrics)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

set.seed(423)
make_features <- function(path) {
df <- readxl::read_excel(path) %>%
# 1) Rename your actual columns to `date` and `index`
dplyr::rename(
date = Dates,
index = `Share Pricing`
) %>%
dplyr::arrange(date) %>% # ensure chronological order
# 2) Compute today's log-return and tomorrow's return as the target
dplyr::mutate(
log_ret = log(index / dplyr::lag(index)),
target = dplyr::lead(log_ret)
) %>%
# 3) For every other column (the ratios), create lag1, lag2, and 5-day rolling mean
dplyr::mutate(across(
-c(date, index, log_ret, target),
list(
lag1 =~dplyr::lag(., 1),
lag2 =~dplyr::lag(., 2),
roll5 =~zoo::rollapplyr(., width = 5, mean, fill = NA)
),
.names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
)) %>%
# 4) Drop any rows missing returns, target, or engineered features
dplyr::filter(!is.na(log_ret), !is.na(target)) %>%
tidyr::drop_na()
# 5) Return:
# - X: matrix of all ratio-based features
# - y: next-day log-return
# - dates: the date vector for plotting
list(
X = df %>% dplyr::select(-date,-index,-log_ret,-target) %>% as.matrix(),
y = df$target,
dates = df$date
)
}
# Apply the function to both TSLA and KO:
tsla <- make_features("TSLA 10-Year Daily Return.xls")
ko <- make_features("KO 10-Year Daily Return.xls")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Train/Test Split & Model Fitting
train_fracs <- c(0.7, 0.8)
all_stats <- list()
# helper: time-based split
split_fun <- function(obj, frac) {
n <- nrow(obj$X)
i <- floor(n * frac)
list(
Xtr = obj$X[1:i, ], ytr = obj$y[1:i],
Xte = obj$X[(i+1):n, ], yte = obj$y[(i+1):n]
)
}
# helper: fit OLS, LASSO, RF; compute MSE, MAE, R2, Corr
fit_eval <- function(Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte) {
# OLS baseline
df_tr <- as.data.frame(Xtr); df_tr$y <- ytr
lm_mod <- lm(y~
., data = df_tr)
p_lm <- predict(lm_mod, newdata = as.data.frame(Xte))
# LASSO with CV
cv_l <- cv.glmnet(Xtr, ytr, alpha = 1)
p_l <- predict(cv_l, s = cv_l$lambda.min, newx = Xte)
# Random Forest
rf_mod <- randomForest(
x = Xtr,
y = ytr,
mtry = floor(sqrt(ncol(Xtr))),
ntree = 500,
maxnodes = 30,
importance = TRUE
)
p_rf <- predict(rf_mod, newdata = Xte)
# collect metrics
tibble::tibble(
Model = c("OLS", "LASSO", "RandomForest"),
MSE = c(mse(yte, p_lm), mse(yte, p_l), mse(yte, p_rf)),
MAE = c(mae(yte, p_lm), mae(yte, p_l), mae(yte, p_rf)),
R2 = c(
1- sum((p_lm- yte)^2)/sum((yte- mean(yte))^2),
1- sum((p_l- yte)^2)/sum((yte- mean(yte))^2),
1- sum((p_rf- yte)^2)/sum((yte- mean(yte))^2)
),
Corr = c(cor(p_lm, yte), cor(p_l, yte), cor(p_rf, yte))
)
}
# loop over train_fracs and symbols
symbols <- list("TSLA" = tsla, "KO" = ko)
for (fr in train_fracs) {
for (name in names(symbols)) {
obj <- symbols[[name]]
sp <- split_fun(obj, fr)
stats <- fit_eval(sp$Xtr, sp$ytr, sp$Xte, sp$yte) %>%
mutate(Symbol = name, Split = fr)
all_stats[[paste(name, fr, sep = "_")]] <- stats
}
}
# combine results
tsla_ko_results <- bind_rows(all_stats)
print(tsla_ko_results)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Visualization
# MSE by Model & Split
ggplot(tsla_ko_results, aes(x = Model, y = MSE, fill = factor(Split))) +
geom_col(position = "dodge") +
facet_wrap(~Symbol) +
labs(
title = "Outâ€‘ofâ€‘Sample MSE by Model & Train/Split (TSLA vs KO)",
x = "Model",
y = "Mean Squared Error (MSE)",
fill = "Train Frac"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# LASSO Improvement over OLS (Î”MSE)
imp <- tsla_ko_results %>%
select(Symbol, Split, Model, MSE) %>%
pivot_wider(
id_cols = c(Symbol, Split),
names_from = Model,
values_from = MSE
) %>%
mutate(Delta = OLS- LASSO)
ggplot(imp, aes(x = factor(Split), y = Delta, color = Symbol, group = Symbol)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "LASSO Improvement over OLS (Î”MSE)",
    x = "Train Fraction",
    y = expression(MSE[OLS]- MSE[LASSO])) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# RÂ˛ Comparison & Î”RÂ˛
# (a) RÂ˛ by Model
ggplot(tsla_ko_results, aes(x = Model, y = R2, fill = factor(Split))) +
geom_col(position = "dodge") +
facet_wrap(~Symbol) +
labs(
title = expression("Outâ€‘ofâ€‘Sample " * R^2 * " by Model & Train/Split"),
x = "Model",
y = expression(R^2),
fill = "Train Frac"
) +
theme_minimal()
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# (b) Î”RÂ˛
imp_R2 <- tsla_ko_results %>%
select(Symbol, Split, Model, R2) %>%
pivot_wider(
id_cols = c(Symbol, Split),
names_from = Model,
values_from = R2
) %>%
mutate(Delta_R2 = LASSO- OLS)
ggplot(imp_R2, aes(x = factor(Split), y = Delta_R2, color = Symbol, group = Symbol)) +
geom_point(size = 3) +
geom_line() +
labs(
title = expression("LASSO Improvement over OLS (" * Delta * R^2 * ")"),
x = "Train Fraction",
y = expression(R^2[LASSO]- R^2[OLS])
) +
theme_minimal()
```
